description: Applying transforms in the wrong order or space so geometry, normals, or lighting are computed in mismatched coordinate systems.
body: Failure mode: a position is multiplied by the projection before the view matrix, or normals are transformed by the model matrix instead of its inverse-transpose, so geometry is misplaced or lighting is wrong while the frame still looks superficially plausible. Detection signals: objects render at unexpected locations or scales; lighting shifts incorrectly as the model is non-uniformly scaled; a reference-image test fails though no error is logged. Do not eyeball a single frame as proof of correct transforms. Safer fallback: label each vector's space, apply model then view then projection in order, transform normals by the inverse-transpose of the model matrix, and verify against a known reference image.
